ParserUtils

Method Detail

fromFile

public static java.lang.CharSequence fromFile(java.lang.String filename)
                                       throws java.io.IOException
Converts the contents of a file into a CharSequence suitable for use by the regex package. example Matcher matcher = pattern.matcher(fromFile("infile.txt"));

Throws:
java.io.IOException

                              java.lang.CharSequence inputStr)

Parameters:
patternStr -
inputStr -
Returns:

removeDuplicateBlanks

public static java.lang.CharSequence removeDuplicateBlanks(java.lang.CharSequence inputStr)
// Returns a version of the input where all contiguous // whitespace characters are replaced with a single // space. Line terminators are treated like whitespace.

Parameters:
inputStr -
Returns:

findFirst

public static java.lang.String findFirst(java.lang.String patternStr,
                                         java.lang.CharSequence input)
Returns the first substring in input that matches the pattern. Returns null if no match found.

Parameters:
patternStr -
input -
Returns:

getLinks

public static java.lang.String[] getLinks(java.lang.String uriStr)
Getting the Links in an HTML Document This method takes a URI which can be either a filename (e.g. file://c:/dir/file.html)or a URL (e.g. http://host.com/page.html) and returns all HREF links in the document.

Parameters:
uriStr -
Returns:

getText

public static java.lang.String getText(java.lang.String uriStr)
// Getting the Text in an HTML Document // This method takes a URI which can be either a filename (e.g. file://c:/dir/file.html) // or a URL (e.g. http://host.com/page.html) and returns all text in the document.

Parameters:
uriStr -
Returns:

escapeRE

public static java.lang.String escapeRE(java.lang.String str)
String patternStr = "i.e."; boolean matchFound = Pattern.matches(patternStr, "i.e.");// true matchFound = Pattern.matches(patternStr, "ibex"); // true // Escape the pattern patternStr = escapeRE(patternStr); // i\.e\. matchFound = Pattern.matches(patternStr, "i.e."); // true matchFound = Pattern.matches(patternStr, "ibex"); // false

readLines

public static java.util.Vector readLines(java.lang.CharSequence inputStr,
                                         boolean keepLineTerminator)
Reading Lines from a String Using a Regular Expression The lines can be terminated with any of the legal line termination character sequences: \r, \r\n, or \n.

return all tags begin with 'tag' no repeated nesting is detected, like if this case happens, only the top tag nesting is added into list.
